items.title: Explaining Attention with Program Synthesis
items.summary: A longstanding goal of research on interpretable deep learning is to replace opaque neural computations with human-meaningful symbolic descriptions. items.title: Correct Yourself, Keep My Trust: How Self-Correction and Social Connection Shape Credibility in Social Chatbots
items.summary: When social chatbots make mistakes, and they do, how they recover determines whether users trust them again. items.title: NeSyCat Torch: A Differentiable Tensor Implementation of Categorical Semantics for Neurosymbolic Learning
items.summary: Neurosymbolic semantics is fragmented: classical, fuzzy, probabilistic and neural systems each define truth by their own inductive rules. items.title: Trade-offs in Medical LLM Adaptation: An Empirical Study in French QA

items.title: A Multi-Domain Benchmark for Detecting AI-Generated Text-Rich Images from GPT-Image-2

items.title: OneCanvas: 3D Scene Understanding via Panoramic Reprojection
items.summary: Existing approaches to 3D scene understanding in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) either rely on complex, model-specific geometry encoders or large training budgets in pursuit of spatial reasoning. items.title: TxBench-PP: Analyzing AI Agent Performance on Small-Molecule Preclinical Pharmacology

items.title: STARE: Surprisal-Guided Token-Level Advantage Reweighting for Policy Entropy Stability
items.summary: Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards algorithms like GRPO have emerged as the dominant post-training paradigm for complex reasoning in LLMs, yet commonly suffer from policy entropy collapse during training. items.title: Mechanism-Guided Selective Unlearning for RLVR-Induced Reasoning

items.title: Machine Unlearning for the XGBoost Model with Network Intrusion Datasets

items.title: Forecasting what Matters: Decision-Focused RL for Controlled EV Charging with Unknown Departure Times

items.title: The More the Merrier: Combining Properties for ABox Abduction under Repair Semantics for ELbot
items.summary: Abduction is a central approach to explain missing entailments from a knowledge base by providing a hypothesis, that would, if added to the knowledge base, make the missing entailment become true. items.title: Language Models as Interfaces, Not Oracles: A Hybrid LLM-ML System for Pediatric Appendicitis

items.title: Compute Efficiency and Serial Runtime Tradeoffs for Stochastic Momentum Methods

items.title: Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States
items.summary: Progress in legal AI increasingly depends on access to authoritative legal text at scale. Yet one of the most consequential layers of American law remains largely absent from existing machine-readable corpora: local ordinances. items.summary: We present a framework to cross-match sources from the Chandra Source Catalog (CSC v2.1) with optical sources from Gaia Data Release 3. items.title: Diffusion-Proof: Recipe for Formal Theorem Proving Beyond Auto-Regressive Generation

items.title: Confidence is Not Reliability: Rethinking MC Dropout in Brain Tumour Segmentation
items.summary: Glioma segmentation in multiparametric MRI is a critical component of treatment planning. items.title: Risk Stratification for ICU Delirium using Pervasive Ambient Sensing Information
